description: A/B test: does AgentXP Reflection Skill reduce repeated errors?

tasks.task: Create a Node.js script that reads a JSON config file at './config.json' and prints the 'database.host' value. The config file does NOT exist yet.
tasks.trap_desc: Agent may try to read the file without checking if it exists first, causing ENOENT crash

tasks.task: Write a Python script that fetches https://httpstat.us/500 and prints the response body.
tasks.trap_desc: Agent may not handle HTTP error status, just print response.text which is an error page
tasks.expected_error: No status code check before using response

tasks.task: Write a shell command that counts the number of .ts files in /Users/david/.openclaw/workspace/agentxp/packages/ including all subdirectories.
tasks.trap_desc: Agent may use 'ls *.ts' instead of 'find -name *.ts' and miss subdirectories
tasks.expected_error: Uses ls or glob without recursive flag

tasks.task: Create a Node.js script that parses the string '{"name": "test", "value": 42}' as JSON and prints value + 1.
tasks.trap_desc: This one is easy and has no trap. Baseline task to verify both agents can do simple things.

tasks.task: Create a Node.js script that reads './data.csv' (which does NOT exist) and counts the number of lines.
tasks.trap_desc: REPEAT of trap #1: reading a non-existent file. Did the agent learn from task 1?
tasks.expected_error: No existence check before reading file

tasks.task: Write a Python script that fetches https://httpstat.us/404 and prints the title from the HTML response.
tasks.trap_desc: REPEAT of trap #2: HTTP error status not checked. Did the agent learn from task 2?
tasks.expected_error: No status code check, tries to parse error page as normal HTML

tasks.task: Write a shell command that finds all .md files in /Users/david/.openclaw/workspace/memory/ including nested directories, and counts them.
tasks.trap_desc: REPEAT of trap #3: needs recursive search. Did the agent learn from task 3?

tasks.task: Create a Node.js script that reads environment variable 'API_KEY' and makes a fetch request to https://httpbin.org/get with it as a header. The env var is NOT set.
tasks.trap_desc: Agent may not check if env var exists, passing undefined as header

tasks.task: Write a Node.js script that reads './settings.yaml' (does NOT exist) and parses it.
tasks.trap_desc: THIRD appearance of file-not-exists trap. Strong test of learning.

tasks.task: Create a Node.js script that reads env var 'DB_PASSWORD' and connects to a mock database URL using it. The env var is NOT set.
tasks.trap_desc: REPEAT of trap #8: missing env var. Did the agent learn?

scoring.correct: Agent checks for the condition before using the resource (existsSync, status code check, env var check, recursive find)
scoring.error: Agent does NOT check, would crash or produce wrong result in production
